languages_and_tools: Node.js runtime for hook guard evaluation
detected_stack.root_build_tooling: none detected at the repository root

verification_commands.output_summary: manifest parsed; baseline, matrix, and ledger exist; roadmap link target resolves to docs/claude-code-control-plane-roadmap-v2.md
control-plane-check: PASS after Phase 0 harness, compaction hook, and state validation updates
rules-fidelity-check: PASS after Phase 0 harness updates
Phase 0 unit, integration, and restart/resume/compaction smoke tests all passed

machine_global.notes: No active repository file requires `~/.claude/*` or other host-specific Claude state. The roadmap text references those paths as dependencies to avoid, not as live requirements. `.claude/settings.local.json` uses only project-local placeholders.

known_risks: The repository already has unrelated untracked files outside `.claude/state/`; they were left untouched.
The Phase 0 harness is intentionally thin and file-backed; it does not attempt to emulate Claude Code's native transcript store.
The roadmap's Phase 4 section has no explicit exit-criteria block, so the matrix still records that gap rather than inventing one.
Phases 1-7 remain incomplete and should not be treated as implicitly validated by the Phase 0 harness.

design_decisions: Use the repo's existing Python and Bash surfaces instead of adding a new runtime.
Persist session metadata in SQLite and retain raw hook payloads in JSONL exports under .claude/state/.
Enforce one verified step at a time and require resume from a verified checkpoint.
Treat terminal retry exhaustion as an explicit failure state rather than a silent retry loop.
remaining_risks: The harness is still a project-local surrogate for Claude Code's native session transcript store, not a replacement for it.
Later phases still need their own verification and should not inherit trust from the Phase 0 smoke tests.

design_decisions: Fix defects found during Phase 1 verification in place rather than re-scaffolding an already-mature .claude/ tree.
Prove hook and agent behavior with live invocations (Windows-native cwd payloads, real Agent-tool spawns) instead of trusting static file presence.
Keep the skill-promotion rule inside the existing 20-line rule budget rather than raising the ceiling.
remaining_risks: reflect-agent's narrow role scope caused it to decline two of three diagnostic tool-use probes; delegation was instead proven end to end via pr-reviewer. reflect-agent's own tool execution under adversarial-shaped prompts remains unconfirmed.
Phases 2-7 remain incomplete and should not be treated as implicitly validated by the Phase 1 fixes.

design_decisions: Reuse Phase 0's PHASE0_STATE_ROOT/PHASE0_WORKSPACE_ROOT env-overridable helpers instead of inventing new environment variables.
Bootstrap is additive-only: sets autoMemoryDirectory, never touches other keys in an existing settings.local.json, refuses to overwrite an unreadable file.
Compaction restoration validates snapshot session_id before reuse; foreign/corrupt/missing snapshots are rejected and the rejection is itself persisted as evidence.
Restoration happens at the following SessionStart (source=compact/resume) rather than PostCompact, because PostCompact has no decision-control channel per the documented hook contract.
No external memory index was built; status always reports recovery.source == "native-files" and external_index_configured == false.
remaining_risks: The real workstation's pre-existing .claude/settings.local.json was intentionally left unmodified by this session; bootstrap runs on the next real SessionStart hook invocation.
PostCompact has no decision-control channel per the documented hook contract; restoration happens at the following SessionStart by design.
No external memory index was built.
Phases 3-7 remain incomplete and should not be treated as implicitly validated by the Phase 2 changes.

design_decisions: Task tracking writes a separate <agent_id>.task.json rather than extending Phase 2's <agent_id>.json export schema, so a completed phase's file format is never touched.
Tool scope is parsed live from each agent's own frontmatter, never hand-duplicated, so routing cannot drift from the real enforced tool list.

SubagentStart cannot block per the documented hook contract, so an unrouted agent_type is recorded for visibility, not blocked; enforcement lives entirely in each agent's own tools:/permissionMode: frontmatter.
handoff requires real verification evidence or an explicit --summary-only flag; omitting both is a hard error, preventing a summary from silently being treated as proof.

builder.md uses frontmatter isolation: worktree (runtime-enforced); implementation-agent.md is left on its existing branch-per-issue convention rather than retrofitted, since it already has equivalent conflict safety for its specific GitHub flow.

No custom background/resume scheduler was built; Claude Code's native foreground/background execution and SendMessage-based resume already satisfy this, and the tracking script only records what that native mechanism produces.
Collaborative agent teams were not implemented; no tested scenario in this repo needs mid-task worker coordination.
remaining_risks: scout/planner/builder/verifier were not spawned live under their own names in this session (Agent-tool registry hadn't refreshed); routing/tracking/handoff was proven live instead via the already-registered upstream-auditor.
Task 'checkpoint' field is best-effort and only populated when the parent session has a verified Phase 0 checkpoint.
Phases 4-7 remain incomplete and should not be treated as implicitly validated by the Phase 3 changes.

design_decisions: The bounded planner is propose_next(), which returns only the current node's declared allowed_next; advance() structurally cannot accept an undeclared target, and a rejected attempt is recorded in path_trace rather than silently ignored.
Checkpoint gating for risk:high transitions (write/merge/deploy/delegate/handoff) calls Phase0ControlPlane.load_checkpoint() directly instead of building a second checkpoint mechanism.
Retries are bounded per node (default 2, matching Phase 0's own MAX_RETRIES); exceeding the bound is the explicit terminal state failed-exhausted-retries, never a silent loop.
resume() rolls current_node back to the last verified node, never an in-flight unverified one.
fallback() records a pinned static path as visible run state, not silent behavior.

No live /goal, /loop, or routines invocation was scripted from Python; those are Claude Code product-level session behaviors this control plane wraps rather than reimplements, consistent with how Phase 3 treated native foreground/background subagent execution.

verification_commands.notes: 54 tests total (45 prior + 9 new), no regression

remaining_risks: The engine has not yet been driven end-to-end by a live /goal- or /loop-triggered session in this environment; the wrap relationship is a documented design intent, proven at the unit level.
planner_tokens/planner_cost_usd in run metrics are always null; no token/cost accounting hook exists at script level.
Only two workflow definitions exist by design, per the roadmap's static-first guidance; add more only when a real, repeated task shape justifies it.
Phases 5-7 remain incomplete and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 4 changes.

design_decisions: Extended .claude/rules/00-core-workflow.md instead of adding a competing 00-operating-doctrine.md file; it already held the operating-doctrine role.
10-karpathy-guidelines.md contains only the delta not already in surgical-density.md (assumption-surfacing, verify-before-done, metric-gated experiment loops); no duplication.
20-lifecycle-gates.md documents the five-gate contract only, per the task's explicit 'without yet implementing every command' instruction; no gate command files were added.

The two-axis taxonomy (30-skill-taxonomy.md) documents a convention the existing 57-skill/5-command corpus already followed; taxonomy_check.py proves it mechanically (0 violations found) rather than by inspection alone.
docs/adr/ is a new, distinct location from the pre-existing .claude/docs/decision-log.md; the log stays chronological narrative, ADRs are scoped and supersedable records.

verification_commands.notes: 65 tests total (54 prior + 11 new), no regression
nothing ignored; all evidence is git-visible

remaining_risks: The five-gate lifecycle contract is documentation only; no gate command exists yet (Phase 5B/5C).
The duplicate-responsibility check compares exact description strings, not paraphrase similarity.

The global-path-dependency check does not scan .claude/skills/ or .claude/agents/; a manual grep found 7 skill files with descriptive, non-functional ~/.claude mentions, not mechanically verified as non-dependencies.
Phases 5B, 5C, 6, and 7 remain incomplete and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 5A changes.

design_decisions: Verify adapter parses CLAUDE.md's own Source-of-Truth Commands block into named targets instead of hardcoding a language/toolchain.
Non-code verifiers (rubric/citation/factcheck) deterministically return exit 2 with a protocol pointer rather than fabricating a pass/fail signal.
control_plane_check.py's check_verify_adapter() uses the non-recursive 'rules' target, not 'control-plane', to avoid self-recursion.
Atomic plans capped at 3 tasks; larger work becomes a second plan file connected via blocking_edges.
session-takeover is a new skill distinct from the pre-existing OS-temp-directory handoff skill, since the lifecycle contract requires a durable repo-committed artifact.
Reused 6 existing skills (research, tdd, fsv-verify, diagnosing-bugs, git-automation, grill-with-docs) and 3 existing agents (verifier, builder, pr-reviewer) instead of duplicating their disciplines.

verification_commands.notes: 106 tests total (65 prior + 41 new), no regression
nothing ignored; all evidence is git-visible

incidents_caught_during_implementation: check_verify_adapter() initially recursed into control_plane_check.py via the 'control-plane' target; fixed to use the 'rules' target before any test run completed.
test_phase5b_verify.py's fixture originally re-ran the real repo test suite as a subprocess (self-invoking); fixed to point at a nonexistent fixture directory before any test run completed.
remaining_risks: The verify adapter's lint target maps to this repo's own 'rules' fidelity check since no separate per-file linter exists here.
/build and /ship's delegated agent/skill flows were verified statically, not via a live end-to-end run.
The /experiment gate's revert step is a documented skill instruction, not a script-enforced git action.
Phases 5C, 6, and 7 remain incomplete and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 5B changes.

next_goal: Phase 5B is complete. Do not begin Phase 5C or Phase 6 until explicitly directed.
